title: Canary rollout latency regression in prod (no auto-mitigation); request SLO-based auto-rollback for model rollouts
preview:
Issue summary
Northstar HR reported elevated tail latency on their Dedicated endpoint during a model canary rollout. The customer observed p95 and p99 latency increases and intermittent client timeouts during peak traffic. The rollout did not automatically pause/rollback, requiring manual intervention by on-call.

C...
text:
Issue summary
Northstar HR reported elevated tail latency on their Dedicated endpoint during a model canary rollout. The customer observed p95 and p99 latency increases and intermittent client timeouts during peak traffic. The rollout did not automatically pause/rollback, requiring manual intervention by on-call.

Customer impact
- Customer-facing latency degradation for chat completions.
- Intermittent client-side timeouts (HTTP 499s observed from the gateway; no increase in 5xx at the platform edge initially).
- The customer explicitly requested an automated safety rail: SLO-based auto-rollback (or auto-pause) for canary/A-B rollouts.

Environment
- Dedicated deployment
- Regions: us-east (primary), eu-west (secondary)
- Route: /v1/chat/completions (tenant: northstar-hr)
- Rollout type: canary traffic shift (10% -> 25% planned)

Timeline (all times PT)
- 2026-02-17 09:42: Rollout st...

title: Audit log pipeline alerts too noisy after rollout (drop-rate/latency) — tune thresholds + update runbook guidance
preview:
Issue Summary
- Since enabling the initial audit log delivery alerts (latency + drop rate), On-Call has been paged repeatedly for short-lived, low-impact fluctuations. The alerts are generating noise and are not aligned with the intended incident thresholds for audit pipeline reliability.

Impact
- Increased on-call...
text:
Issue Summary
- Since enabling the initial audit log delivery alerts (latency + drop rate), On-Call has been paged repeatedly for short-lived, low-impact fluctuations. The alerts are generating noise and are not aligned with the intended incident thresholds for audit pipeline reliability.

Impact
- Increased on-call load and alert fatigue for #eng-oncall / #eng-sre.
- Several pages triggered during expected/benign conditions (brief exporter backpressure, transient ingestion backlog recoveries, low-volume tenants).
- Risk: real incidents may get ignored or delayed due to repeated false positives.

What we’re seeing
- Drop-rate alert firing during short ingestion backlogs or during exporter retries, even when events are later delivered (at-least-once semantics).
- Latency alert firing on p95 spikes during low traffic periods, especially in regions with fewer events.
- Alerts firing per-...

Issue summary:
Northstar reports Optimize config changes in eu-west are getting stuck in "Gate running" and then failing with a timeout. Because gating is required for apply, changes remain blocked.

Customer impact:

- No production traffic regression reported; this is a control-plane / eval execution reliability issue.

Environment:
- Hosted API workloads
- Region: eu-west
- Routes: northstar-prod-search (primary), northstar-prod-support-chat (secondary)

What the customer sees:

text:
Issue summary
- During a live SE-led demo for Northstar Payments, the Redwood demo tenant began returning HTTP 429 responses mid-flow (streaming chat + tool call step). This caused the demo to stall and required switching to backup screenshots for the Console section.

Customer impact
- Live demo interruption (~6–8 minutes). Customer saw repeated 429s and intermittent retries.
- Risk: undermines reliability story and raises concern about quota predictability/noisy-neighbor.

When / where
- Date/time: 2025-11-12 ~19:10–19:25 UTC
- Environment: prod / us-east
- Tenant: Redwood internal demo org ("demo tenant")

Observed behavior
- Chat/completions requests started returning:
 - 429 Too Many Requests
 - Response headers included rate-limit remaining=0 (per-minute)
- Retries from demo app increased request volume, exacerbating throttling.

title: Latency regression after runtime-1.22 canary: elevated p99 decode latency correlated with KV compaction activity
preview:
Issue Summary
- During a runtime-1.22 canary expansion in prod (hosted, us-east-1), we observed a statistically significant p99 latency regression on decode-heavy traffic, most visible for mixed sequence-length distributions.
- Regression correlated with elevated KV compaction work (bytes moved + compaction time) an...
text:
Issue Summary
- During a runtime-1.22 canary expansion in prod (hosted, us-east-1), we observed a statistically significant p99 latency regression on decode-heavy traffic, most visible for mixed sequence-length distributions.
- Regression correlated with elevated KV compaction work (bytes moved + compaction time) and increased allocator fragmentation ratio oscillation.
- Canary was rolled back (feature flag disabled + cohort drained) per guardrails.

Customer Impact
- Customer: Northwind Analytics (enterprise) reported increased p99 latency and intermittent request timeouts during bursty chat traffic.
- Symptoms: p95 largely stable; p99 degraded (notable tail spikes) during bursts and after churn events (rapid session start/stop).
- Scope: Only canary cohort in us-east-1. Control region (eu-west-1) did not show comparable change.

Detection
- 2026-01-29 09:12 PT: SRE alert fired: host...

Issue summary
- Customer reports a noticeable tail-latency regression after a runtime upgrade on their Dedicated deployment.
- Correlates with internal Q1 baseline sweep signals: a similar p99 TTFT regression signature appeared in perf-canary for long-prefill + mixed chat workloads, but was initially classified as canary-only/no-customer-impact until this report.

Customer impact
- HelioBank reports increased time-to-first-token (TTFT) and overall response time on streaming chat endpoints.
- Impact: degraded UX (customer-facing assistant), increased client-side timeouts, and higher retry volume.
- Customer states this started “within the last ~24 hours” following their scheduled runtime upgrade window.

Timeline (customer-provided)
- 2026-01-18 21:00–23:00 UTC: Dedicated runtime upgrade applied (customer acknowledged maintenance window).
- 2026-01-19 07:30 UTC: First internal alert fr...

Issue summary

- This blocks rollback during gameday / incident response and is confusing because the operator is in the expected on-call group.

- Risk: delayed mitigation if a bad optimization config causes latency/5xx regression.

Who is affected
- Internal users in Okta/SSO group: sre-oncall (expected to have Optimization Change Operator privileges).

Observed behavior
- UI: No "Rollback" CTA shown on version detail page.
- API: direct call to rollback endpoint returns 403.

Expected behavior
- Members of the on-call operator group should have permission to rollback optimization configs within approved scopes.

Request type: IAM / service account permissions

- Ability for svc-perf-canary to call internal inference endpoints in prod for a curated allowlist of models.

text:
Issue summary
- The benchmark fleet runner service account cannot (a) create/run GPU benchmark jobs reliably and (b) export run artifacts (manifests, traces, JSON results) to the shared artifacts bucket.
- This started after the benchmark fleet moved to Workload Identity + a new namespace split for perf-canary vs ad-hoc runs.

Impact
- Blocks Q1 2026 perf baseline sweeps and perf-canary nightly runs from producing publishable artifacts.
- Engineers can sometimes run locally, but CI and scheduled runs fail, leading to missing data in dashboards and delayed regression triage.

Who is affected
- Serving Runtime benchmarking owners + SRE perf-canary pipeline.

Environment
- Benchmark fleet Kubernetes clusters: bench-us-east (primary), bench-eu-west (secondary)
- Namespace(s): benchmark-lab, perf-canary
- Runner identity:
 - KSA: perf-runner (namespace: perf-canary)
 - KSA: benchmark-runne...

Issue summary:

What changed:

title: Demo tenant: SSE streaming disconnects intermittently in eu-west during live demos
preview:
Issue summary:
During live demos using the demo tenant in eu-west, Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming for chat completions intermittently disconnects mid-response. Customers see partial output followed by a client-side error (connection closed / EOF) and no final usage. This is demo-impacting and has occurred twice ...
text:
Issue summary:
During live demos using the demo tenant in eu-west, Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming for chat completions intermittently disconnects mid-response. Customers see partial output followed by a client-side error (connection closed / EOF) and no final usage. This is demo-impacting and has occurred twice in the last week when demos were intentionally run in eu-west for data residency narrative.

Impact:
- SE cannot reliably demonstrate streaming + retries + tool calling on eu-west.
- Workaround exists (switch demo to us-east), but undermines the EMEA/data residency talk track.
- No evidence of data loss; this is transport-level disconnect. However, it breaks the “production reliability” narrative.

Customer context:
Summit Telecom (EMEA enterprise prospect) requested eu-west demo due to data residency concerns. Disconnect happened live during the streaming portion of the ta...

title: Retention policy TTL not being applied to request/response logs in prod us-east (purge lag > configured TTL)
preview:
Issue summary:
Northbridge Bank reports that their configured retention policy for request/response logs (TTL = 7 days) does not appear to be enforced in us-east. They are seeing request log entries older than 10 days still present in the Dedicated tenant.

Impact:
- Customer is in an active enterprise security revi...
text:
Issue summary:
Northbridge Bank reports that their configured retention policy for request/response logs (TTL = 7 days) does not appear to be enforced in us-east. They are seeing request log entries older than 10 days still present in the Dedicated tenant.

Impact:
- Customer is in an active enterprise security review and needs evidence that retention controls are enforced.
- Operational impact: risk of non-compliance with customer policy until backlog is cleared.
- No serving/runtime impact reported; this is a data lifecycle / purge enforcement issue.

Customer statement (paraphrased):
"We set request log retention to 7 days last week. In us-east we can still query request logs from 10–12 days ago. Is TTL actually running?"

Environment:
- Product: Dedicated
- Region: prod us-east
- Data class: request/response logs (NOT audit logs)
- Customer policy: request_logs.ttl_days=7, prompts...

title: Provision dedicated GPU benchmark runner capacity for Runtime LTS perf gates (avoid blocking CI)
preview:
Request
- We need dedicated GPU runner capacity for perf-canary benchmarks used as *release gates* for Runtime LTS patch releases.
- Current state: perf jobs are contending with general CI and ad-hoc benchmark runs, causing queue times and occasional partial coverage during patch week.

Why this matters
- LTS patche...
text:
Request
- We need dedicated GPU runner capacity for perf-canary benchmarks used as *release gates* for Runtime LTS patch releases.
- Current state: perf jobs are contending with general CI and ad-hoc benchmark runs, causing queue times and occasional partial coverage during patch week.

Why this matters
- LTS patches are blocked on perf gate results (p50/p95 latency + throughput across canonical workloads). Any runner starvation delays releases and increases risk of releasing without full coverage.
- We also need to avoid impacting regular developer CI and nightly runs.

Scope
- Provision/allocate a separate runner pool (or reserved quota) for perf-canary “release-branch / LTS gate” workflows.
- Ensure capacity exists for concurrent runs during patch week (RC validation + reruns for flakes) without starving mainline.

Requirements (from Runtime LTS workstream)
- GPU SKUs: H100 (primar...

title: Request: official backup/restore + DR runbook for Redwood Private (VPC) deployment (FinBank)

text:
Issue summary:
FinBank (Private / VPC) is requesting an official, supportable backup/restore runbook for Redwood Private control plane and a DR checklist suitable for internal audit. They are in late-stage security + reliability validation and need a customer-facing guidance doc (or an approved interim runbook) to proceed.

Customer ask / urgency:
- Provide "official" Redwood guidance for: (1) consistent backups, (2) restore into a clean cluster, (3) DR procedure (single-region) with validation steps.
- Confirm what is included/excluded (Kubernetes objects vs Redwood control plane state; secrets handling).
- Provide encryption + key management expectations (customer-managed KMS key, rotation expectations, behavior if KMS unavailable).
- Provide recommended storage backend patterns (S3 + KMS + least privilege IAM).
- Provide evidence artifacts they can keep for audit (backup manifest, ...

title: EU-pinned customer seeing cross-region (US) fallback attempt when EU capacity constrained
preview:
Issue summary:
Nordholm Payments reports that after enabling EU-only region pinning for their Hosted API project, they observed at least one request attempting to fall back to a US region when EU capacity was constrained. They require strict enforcement (no cross-region routing/fallback).

Impact:
- Compliance risk ...
text:
Issue summary:
Nordholm Payments reports that after enabling EU-only region pinning for their Hosted API project, they observed at least one request attempting to fall back to a US region when EU capacity was constrained. They require strict enforcement (no cross-region routing/fallback).

Impact:
- Compliance risk perception for customer (regulated payments).
- Customer paused POC traffic until confirmed fixed.

Customer expectation:
- Requests must be served only within the configured allowed regions (EU-only).
- If EU is unavailable/capacity constrained, the request must be blocked with an explicit residency violation error (not retried in US).

Environment:
- Prod
- Customer config: project pinned to EU-only; primary region: eu-west
- Observed behavior: smart router selected eu-west then attempted us-east fallback

Steps to reproduce (from customer):

title: Enable Hosted data residency enforcement feature flag in staging + seed test orgs/API keys for verification

text:
Background
We are preparing end-to-end staging verification for Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning + deterministic blocks + standardized errors). This internal support request is to enable the staging feature flags and seed a few test orgs/projects/API keys so Engineering + SRE + Security can validate routing, fallback behavior, logging boundaries, and the public error contract.

- Enable residency policy evaluation + enforcement in gateway + routing.
- Ensure cross-region fallback is disabled by default when residency constraints are present.

2) Seed staging test entities
Create the following in staging control plane (do not use real customer data):

- Project: "hosted-chat"
- Residency policy: allowed_regions=[EU], primary_region=EU (...

title: Staging: Private control-plane backup CronJob failing with RBAC "forbidden" (cannot list/get secrets + Redwood CRs)

Impact

text:
Issue summary
- The scheduled backup CronJob deployed via Helm in staging is failing immediately due to missing RBAC permissions.
- This blocks our nightly backup verification and is causing noise for on-call.

Impact
- Staging backup schedule is effectively disabled (no successful backups since enabling the chart values).
- No customer impact (staging only), but this blocks Day-2 ops hardening and restore drill automation.

Environment
- Cluster: private-staging-us-east-1
- Namespace: redwood-private
- Chart: redwood-private (backup CronJob enabled)
- Backup storage backend: S3 (staging bucket)

Symptoms
- CronJob pods crash with "forbidden" errors when attempting to enumerate resources required for control-plane backup.

Steps to reproduce
1) Deploy/upgrade Helm release with backup enabled:
 - values.backup.enabled=true
 - values.backup.schedule="0 2 * * *"
2) Wait for CronJob to cr...
